learning_outcomes.description: Analyse and evaluate accounting, financial and non-financial information relevant to the task of asset allocation and security selection.
Assess the value of a financial asset using a variety of accepted methods.
Explain and evaluate the risks associated with investments on portfolio and security levels.
Evaluate the performance of a portfolio and portfolio manager.
Apply theory to the analysis of real world cases when working as part of a team, and employ databases and software commonly used in the industry.
Identify ethical dilemmas in various investment scenarios and explain the potential conflicts of interest that may arise in the investment industry.

assessments.due_date:
Quiz 1 - Week 5, Fri 5:00 pm

Quiz 2 - Week 12, Fri 5:00 pm
assessments.task_description: Students will complete two 24-hour quizzes consisting of multiple-choice, modelling (quantitative/coding), and discussion questions.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Simulated Portfolio - Part A - Pitch Deck, Presentation and Peer Assessment
assessments.due_date:
Pitch Deck - Friday 5:00PM - Week 9 Fri

Presentation During Tutorial Class - Week 8 - Week 9

Peer Evaluation - 5:00 PM - Week 10 Fri
assessments.task_description:
In this assessment, you will step into the role of a Fund Manager reporting to an Investment Committee. Drawing on the data generated during the Live Trading Simulation.

You are required to deliver a professional presentation that defends your investment decisions and critically reflects on the outcomes.

Part A Format:

In-Person Presentation: A professional pitch covering your investment philosophy, strategy execution, and performance attribution.

15 mins (10 min presentation + 5 min Q&A)

All team members must contribute to the oral presentation and/or Q&A

Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.

Upon completing the team project, you must also complete the Peer Assessment. The results of Peer Assessment may be used to adjust your individual marks according to your contribution.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Part B Format:

Reflection on the Trading Game (Written ~1000 words): This component requires you to critically evaluate your simulation experience by contrasting academic financial theory with the realities of practical portfolio management.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The final assessment consists of

  • Short-answer questions,
  • Essay questions.

Further details will be discussed in class.

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 3: Multifactor models: APT, ICAPM

This topic discusses macroeconomic and industry analysis which is particularly useful in timing factor investments. It introduces a wide range of multi-factor asset pricing models to address the empirical challenges of the CAPM.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L05
